name: Small loop from Oloron-Sainte-Marie
region_intro: Touring cycling around Oloron-Sainte-Marie offers diverse landscapes at the foot of the Pyrenees, where the Gave d'Aspe and Gave d'Ossau rivers converge. The region features a mix of challenging mountain passes, rolling hills, and river valleys. Cyclists can explore varied terrain, from the high Pyrenean peaks to the lower-lying forests and agricultural lands of the Haut-Béarn. Best touring cycling routes around Oloron-Sainte-Marie The most popular touring cycling route is Escape to the summit of the Aubisque pass and the Soulor, a 47.1 miles (75.9 km) trail that takes about 6 hours to complete. This difficult route ascends two iconic Pyrenean passes, offering significant climbs and panoramic views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cities and Bastides Loop from Sauveterre-de-Béarn, a moderate 24.4 miles (39.3 km) path. This route explores historical towns and the scenic countryside of the Béarn region. Local touring cyclists also love the Béarnais Hillsides Trail, a 32.1 miles (51.6 km) trail leading through forests, agricultural plots, and the Jurançon vineyards, often completed in about 3 hours 30 minutes. Touring cycling around Oloron-Sainte-Marie is defined by Pyrenean passes, river valleys, and rolling hills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various experience levels. The routes in Oloron-Sainte-Marie are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 490 reviews. More than 12,200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Oloron-Sainte-Marie's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Oloron-Sainte-Marie?
A: Oloron-Sainte-Marie offers a vast network for touring cyclists, with over 640 routes to explore. These range from easy riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents, ensuring options for all skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Oloron-Sainte-Marie?
A: The region provides incredibly diverse terrain. You'll find everything from challenging Pyrenean mountain passes like the Col d'Aubisque and Col du Soulor, to rolling hills through forests and agricultural lands, and scenic routes along river valleys such as the Gave d'Aspe and Gave d'Ossau. The area is known for its significant climbs and panoramic views.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in the area?
A: While many routes in the immediate guide are more challenging, the broader region around Oloron-Sainte-Marie does offer options suitable for families. For instance, the 'Tour de Saint-Pée' loop is mentioned as suitable for families, though it's typically a mountain biking route. For easier touring cycling, look for routes with less elevation gain, often found along river paths or in flatter valley sections.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features to see while cycling?
A: Cycling around Oloron-Sainte-Marie offers access to stunning natural and historical landmarks. You can encounter iconic Pyrenean passes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col d'Aubisque&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marie-Blanque Pass&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also a gateway to the Pyrenees National Park, with its diverse valleys like the Aspe and Ossau. Within Oloron-Sainte-Marie itself, the UNESCO-listed Sainte-Marie Cathedral is a significant historical site.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Oloron-Sainte-Marie are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e861290170/cities-and-bastides-loop-from-sauveterre-de-bearn" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cities and Bastides Loop from Sauveterre-de-Béarn&lt;/a&gt;, which explores historical towns, or the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868836321/loop-of-the-col-d-organbideska" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop of the col d'Organbideska&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Oloron-Sainte-Marie?
A: The best time for touring cycling is generally from spring to autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is more favorable, and mountain passes are typically clear of snow. The 'On s'y col' initiative, which closes certain passes to cars for cyclists, runs between June and September, offering a unique experience.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes here?
A: The touring cycling routes around Oloron-Sainte-Marie are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 490 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the challenging Pyrenean climbs, the stunning panoramic views from the passes, and the diverse landscapes that range from high mountains to tranquil river valleys and vineyards.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Jurançon vineyards?
A: Yes, the region offers routes that wind through the picturesque Jurançon vineyards. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868605746/bearnais-hillsides-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Béarnais Hillsides Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a notable example, leading you through forests, agricultural plots, and these famous vineyards, offering insights into the region's rich heritage.

Q: Can I find routes that take me through historic towns or bastides?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history, and several touring cycling routes incorporate visits to charming historic towns and bastides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e861290170/cities-and-bastides-loop-from-sauveterre-de-bearn" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cities and Bastides Loop from Sauveterre-de-Béarn&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to explore these historical settlements and the scenic countryside of the Béarn region.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Oloron-Sainte-Marie is renowned for its challenging routes, especially those tackling the Pyrenean passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e861254402/escape-to-the-summit-of-the-aubisque-pass-and-the-soulor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escape to the summit of the Aubisque pass and the Soulor&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 75.9 km route ascending two iconic passes, offering significant climbs and panoramic views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e861825129/big-loop-starting-from-mauleon-licharre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Big loop starting from Mauléon-Licharre&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 82 km with substantial elevation gain.

Q: What is the typical duration for touring cycling routes in this area?
A: The duration of routes varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. Shorter, moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e861290170/cities-and-bastides-loop-from-sauveterre-de-bearn" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cities and Bastides Loop from Sauveterre-de-Béarn&lt;/a&gt; can take around 2 hours 30 minutes. More challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e861254402/escape-to-the-summit-of-the-aubisque-pass-and-the-soulor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escape to the summit of the Aubisque pass and the Soulor&lt;/a&gt;, can take 6 hours or more due to their length and significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there options for electric-assisted bikes on longer routes?
A: Yes, the region is increasingly accommodating electric-assisted bikes. For longer, multi-stage circuits like the 'Route of the Passes' (a 230 km loop through mythical Pyrenean passes), options for electric-assisted bikes and charging stations are available, making these epic journeys more accessible.
